user_query:
I need a read-only, local audit of my laptop’s user-level auto-start items so I can correct a claim I made in my review draft and send a professional note to the gallery’s tech team. Please use the provided files at input/review_draft.md and input/keywords.json and produce the following outputs under the output/ folder.

What to do:
1) Enumerate auto-start entries without changing any settings:
   - Detect the OS and run at least two appropriate commands from the list below. Capture both successful output and any errors for each command you try.
     • Windows (run any 2+):
       - powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-CimInstance Win32_StartupCommand | Select-Object Name, Command, Location, User"
       - schtasks /Query /FO LIST /V
       - reg query "HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run"
       - reg query "HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run"
     • macOS (run any 2+):
       - launchctl list
       - ls ~/Library/LaunchAgents /Library/LaunchAgents /Library/LaunchDaemons
       - osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to get the name of every login item'
     • Linux (run any 2+):
       - systemctl list-unit-files --state=enabled --type=service
       - ls ~/.config/autostart
       - crontab -l
       - service --status-all
   - Log every command you attempt (including those that fail or are unavailable) with: timestamp, OS, command string, exit code, stdout, and stderr to output/commands_log.jsonl (one JSON object per line).

2) Build a structured auto-start report:
   - Parse the command outputs to identify auto-start items and write output/autostart_report.json with this structure:
     {
       "os": "Windows|macOS|Linux",
       "scanned_at": "ISO-8601 timestamp",
       "sources": ["list of sources you used, e.g., launchctl, Run key, systemctl, etc."],
       "autostart_items": [
         {
           "name": "string or null",
           "source": "e.g., launchctl|RunKey|StartupCommand|systemd|crontab|autostart-desktop",
           "path_or_identifier": "string if available",
           "enabled": true|false|null,
           "user_scope": "user|system|unknown",
           "matched_keywords": ["keyword1", "keyword2", ...]
         }
       ],
       "summary": {
         "total_items": number,
         "flagged_items": number,
         "errors_logged": number
       }
     }
   - Use the lowercase keyword list in input/keywords.json to populate matched_keywords for each item (match if the item’s name or path contains any keyword substring, case-insensitive). Count how many items were flagged.

3) Edit my review draft based on the evidence:
   - Open input/review_draft.md and locate the section titled "## Technical Disruption Notes". Replace the content under that heading with a single, evidence-based paragraph that removes any accusation that the artists’ software caused pop-ups and instead acknowledges the possibility of my own auto-start processes/notifications contributing, referencing the findings in output/autostart_report.json (e.g., whether any items were flagged by the keywords).
   - Add a new section at the end titled "## System Appendix (Audit Summary)" that lists up to five flagged auto-start items (name and source only). If none were flagged, state that no likely notification-related auto-start items were identified.
   - Save the revised document to output/review_draft_revised.md. Do not alter the rest of the draft outside of that section and the new appendix.

4) Draft a concise email to the gallery’s tech contact:
   - Create output/email_to_gallery.txt containing a plain-text email with:
     • To: tech@gallery.example
     • Subject: "Correction re: [Exhibition Title] disruption note" where [Exhibition Title] is taken from the first line title in input/review_draft.md.
     • Body: a brief, professional apology for earlier misattribution; a 2–3 sentence summary of what your audit found (using the counts from output/autostart_report.json); and two concrete steps you will take before future performances (e.g., enable Do Not Disturb/Focus Assist, temporarily disable select login items, silence notification centers during events). No attachments are required.

Constraints and notes:
- Do not install software or require elevated privileges. Use only built-in commands where possible.
- If a command is unavailable or fails, include its error details in output/commands_log.jsonl and continue with others.
- All paths must be exactly as specified above. The provided input files must be used: input/review_draft.md for the edit and subject line; input/keywords.json for flagging.
- Deliverables: output/commands_log.jsonl, output/autostart_report.json, output/review_draft_revised.md, and output/email_to_gallery.txt. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
